behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is the model producing a wrong signature from natural language; stubbed outputs can only test checker logic, not generation quality. Rejected Workflow because behavior text only requires one generation step, not a multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real LLM on a prompt that specifies an exact typed signature, then assert the produced function signature exactly matches expected parameter names/types and return type.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly spans iterative agentic steps (initial generation plus possible refinement attempts) and depends on real model output quality across attempts. Rejected Agentic because single-step testing does not cover the 'subsequent attempt' success path in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full retry loop with a real LLM and real test execution, using a task where first attempt often fails but a later attempt can pass, and assert the service reports success when any attempt passes.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: test-runner output must be propagated into prompt-construction for next attempt. Rejected System because real OS/network/subprocess behavior is not required to verify inclusion; rejected Agentic because correctness here is prompt content wiring, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real refinement/prompt-building modules with a deterministic failing test result payload and capture the next LLM request body, asserting failure messages are included verbatim or by required template fields.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-state orchestration and error-shape aggregation across modules; deterministic stubs for generation and test execution can expose real logic failures. Rejected System because no real OS/network primitive is required; rejected Agentic because model quality is not under test, only control flow and result collation.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator + persistence/response modules with forced-fail attempt outcomes for three rounds, then assert loop termination at attempt 3 and structured error includes all three attempt artifacts and test results.

behaviors.description: The full loop — generate, test, refine, test again — produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a multi-step agent pipeline where later correctness depends on earlier model output plus test feedback. Rejected Agentic because a single model-step test cannot validate the end-to-end generate→test→refine sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a benchmark function known to usually fail in one-shot prompting, run the full service loop with real LLM calls and test execution, and assert the final attempt yields a passing implementation within retry budget.
